trivia_bullets.text: Colorless common Artifact Creature — Golem from Core Set 2021 (July 3, 2020). Previewed June 16, 2020, by Wizards of the Coast directly. Fills the draft format's colorless splash slot — core sets historically include at least one colorless artifact creature common for this purpose.

"It was built to withstand the ages, but no one remembers who built it or why it keeps its eternal watch." — no speaker. The amnesia theme mirrors the card's mechanical purpose: a generic draft body with no plane-specific identity, just as the golem has no known creator or mission.

4/3 Golem for four colorless mana, enters tapped. Draft chassis: reasonable body any deck can splash, held back by enters-tapped to prevent aggressive use. EDHREC rank 29,136 — near bottom of tracked cards, consistent with draft filler that sees no meaningful Commander play. Legal in Pauper, Paupercommander, Pioneer, Modern, Legacy, Vintage.

Art by Joe Slucher, freelance illustrator with Magic credits. Texture-heavy, naturalist creature style — the weathered rust-and-moss golem is characteristic of his approach. No Hipsters of the Coast spotlight found for this card or artist.
